q.en: What award do third graders earn by climbing the rope to the top two times in a month?
q.zh: 三年级学生在一个月内把绳子爬到顶两次，能获得什么奖？

options.en: The Math Wizard Award according to some versions of the story
options.zh: “数学高手”奖（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Coach Mack announces, 'All third graders that climb to the top two times this month will receive the Twice-Fit Mice Award.'
explain.zh: 麦克教练宣布：“所有在一个月内爬到顶两次的三年级学生，都将获得‘两次达标’鼠奖。”

q.en: Instead of practicing the rope climb, how did Morty and his friends spend their afternoons?
q.zh: 莫蒂和朋友们没有练习爬绳，而是把下午的时间花在了什么上？

options.en: Helping classmates train according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 帮助同学训练（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Playing video games like Mega Mouse Avenger
options.zh: 玩《超级鼠复仇者》之类的电子游戏

explain.en: The book says they 'spent their afternoons playing video games,' including Morty's new Mega Mouse Avenger game.
explain.zh: 书中说他们“整个下午都在玩电子游戏”，包括莫蒂新买的《超级鼠复仇者》。

q.en: What does 'arrogance' mean in the story?
q.zh: 在故事中，“arrogance（傲慢）”是什么意思？

options.en: An insulting way of thinking of oneself as better than others
options.zh: 一种自认为比别人高明、更重要的无礼想法

options.en: A feeling of fear according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 一种恐惧的感觉（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The glossary defines 'arrogance' as 'an insulting way of thinking of oneself as better or more important than others.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将“arrogance”定义为“一种自认为比别人高明或重要的无礼想法”。

q.en: What happened when Morty tried his second climb on the final day?
q.zh: 在最后一天，莫蒂尝试第二次攀爬时发生了什么？

options.en: His paws slipped and he dropped to the mat without reaching the top
options.zh: 他的爪子打滑，没爬到顶就掉到了垫子上

options.en: He reached the top easily according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 他轻松地爬到了顶（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: Morty 'hadn't practiced once since his first climb,' his paws slipped, and 'He loosened his grip and dropped to the mat.'
explain.zh: 莫蒂“自第一次攀爬后再没练过”，爪子打滑，“他松开手掉到了垫子上”。

q.en: Based on the story, why did Morty fail his second climb?
q.zh: 根据故事内容，莫蒂第二次攀爬为什么失败了？

options.en: Because he had not practiced since his first climb while his friends had
options.zh: 因为自第一次攀爬后他就没有练习，而朋友们练了

options.en: Because Marta pulled him down according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 因为玛尔塔把他拉了下来（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Because the rope was too short

options.zh: 因为麦克教练阻止了他

explain.en: The book states Morty 'hadn't practiced once since his first climb' and 'Watched his friends struggle was scaring him,' unlike Ben and Fred who kept practicing.
explain.zh: 书中说莫蒂“自第一次攀爬后再没练过”，而本和弗雷德一直在练习，所以朋友们能爬上去。

q.en: What lesson does Morty learn by the end of the story?
q.zh: 在故事结尾，莫蒂明白了什么道理？

options.en: Being a good sport and helping others matters more than showing off
options.zh: 做个好运动员、帮助他人，比炫耀自己更重要

options.en: Video games make you strong according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 电子游戏能让你变强壮（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: Morty realizes 'these mice were very good sports, and that he was not,' and he apologizes for his arrogance, showing the author's lesson.
explain.zh: 莫蒂意识到“这些老鼠才是真正的好运动员，而他自己不是”，并为自己的傲慢道歉，体现了作者的寓意。

q.en: After failing the climb, what did Morty do to make up for his behavior?
q.zh: 攀爬失败后，莫蒂为了弥补自己的所作所为做了什么？

options.en: He ignored everyone according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他对所有人不予理睬（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: He apologized to Marta and agreed to help her with soccer
options.zh: 他向玛尔塔道歉，并答应帮她练足球

explain.en: Morty approached Marta and said, 'I'm sorry for the way I acted... you cheered me on anyway,' and agreed to help her with soccer.
explain.zh: 莫蒂找到玛尔塔说：“我为我的行为道歉……你却还为我加油，”并答应帮她练足球。

q.en: What does 'chafed' mean when describing Morty's paws?
q.zh: 形容莫蒂的爪子时，“chafed”是什么意思？

options.en: Sore, raw, or damaged from rubbing
options.zh: 因摩擦而酸痛、破皮或受损

options.en: Clean and smooth according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 干净又光滑（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The glossary defines 'chafed' as 'sore, raw, or damaged from rubbing,' as when Morty's paws were chafed after climbing.
explain.zh: 词汇表将“chafed”定义为“因摩擦而酸痛、破皮或受损”，比如莫蒂爬绳后爪子被磨伤。

q.en: How did the other mice react when Morty dropped from the rope?
q.zh: 当莫蒂从绳子上掉下来时，其他老鼠是什么反应？

options.en: They took away his award according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他们没收了他的奖（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: They cheered him and said 'Nice effort, Morty!'
options.zh: 他们为他加油，并说“努力了，莫蒂！”

explain.en: The book says the other mice 'brushed over his failure' and shouted, 'Nice effort, Morty!' showing good sportsmanship.
explain.zh: 书中说其他老鼠“不去计较他的失败”，并大喊“努力了，莫蒂！”，表现了良好的体育精神。

q.en: How many climbs to the top were needed to earn the Twice-Fit Mice Award?
q.zh: 要获得“两次达标”鼠奖，需要爬到顶多少次？

explain.en: The award required climbing 'to the top two times this month,' as Coach Mack explains at the start.
explain.zh: 正如麦克教练一开始所说，获得该奖需要“在这个月内爬到顶两次”。

prompt.en: Think of a time you were too proud or made fun of someone. How could you show better sportsmanship like the mice at the end?
prompt.zh: 想一想你曾经过于骄傲或取笑过别人的时刻。你能怎样像结尾的老鼠们那样表现出更好的体育精神？

en: Recall how Marta cheered for Morty even after he was mean to her.
Think about what it means to be a 'good sport' when others fail.
zh: 回忆一下，玛尔塔在莫蒂对她很刻薄之后，仍然为他加油。
想一想当别人失败时，做一个“好运动员”意味着什么。
